player_choice_log.text: I don't need to justify myself to a door guard. We're fighting the same war. Open the door.
I pushed too hard. Look — I need in. What do you actually need from me?
You've been testing me since I walked up. Fine. But you and I are fighting the same war. Either let me prove that, or we both lose.

conversation.topic: Assess and either admit or turn away this stranger

history.speaker: Player
history.text: I don't need to justify myself to a door guard. We're fighting the same war. Open the door.

history.speaker: Troy
history.text: You've got some nerve, stranger. This isn't about your credentials here.

thought.internal_thought: I stopped pretending there's a third option a long time ago.
thought.subjective_belief: The situation doesn't have room for ambiguity right now.

interaction_outcome.max_response: You picked the wrong door to be rude at.

history.speaker: Player
history.text: I pushed too hard. Look — I need in. What do you actually need from me?

history.speaker: Troy
history.text: I don't doubt your bravery, but we're still on different sides. Let's proceed with caution.

thought.internal_thought: The framing gives them away — they see the world like I do.
thought.subjective_belief: Their worldview aligns with mine — the approach confirms it.

interaction_outcome.min_response: Too late. Should've led with that.

history.speaker: Player
history.text: You've been testing me since I walked up. Fine. But you and I are fighting the same war. Either let me prove that, or we both lose.

terminal_outcome.result: Guard refuses entry. Player is turned away.
terminal_outcome.final_dialogue: We have a deal on terms. Otherwise, this conversation ends here.

world.personal_history: Troy is one of the earliest members of the Insurgency. He is not a founder of the insurgency, nor was he a higher-up when he first joined but he witnessed countless insurgent deaths in the more chaotic early RI conflicts. He is devout loyal to Moses. He might question his logic, but he doesn't question his heart.
